name: urlshortener
description: A standard-library-only URL shortener HTTP service using net/http ServeMux (Go 1.22+ method+wildcard routing) and encoding/json. POST /shorten accepts {"url"} and returns {"code"} (201) after validating the URL is an absolute http(s) URL; GET /{code} issues a 302 redirect to the stored target; unknown codes return 404. Storage is an in-memory, RWMutex-guarded map that generates random base62 codes. Verified green: gofmt-clean, go build, go vet, and go test (including -race) all pass.

maintain.request: Add a stats endpoint: GET /api/stats/{code} returns JSON {"code","url","hits"} with the stored target URL and a redirect hit counter, and every successful GET /{code} redirect increments that code's hit count. Unknown codes return 404.

review.bug: Store.Put acquires a read lock (RLock) instead of a write lock (Lock) while inserting into the codes map, so concurrent shorten requests cause a data race and can trigger a fatal concurrent map write.
